topics.event: AI deciphers Herculaneum scrolls
topics.summary: A team of student researchers won the Vesuvius Challenge by using machine learning to read passages from carbonized scrolls buried by the 79 AD eruption of Mount Vesuvius. The recovered text appears to be a previously unread Greek philosophical work.

topics.event: KitKat truck heist in Germany
topics.summary: Thieves stole a tractor-trailer holding around 20 tons of KitKat bars on a German highway in early 2024. The case became an internet sensation, prompting jokes about a black market for the chocolate and serious questions about how easily a truck full of brand-name candy can disappear.

topics.event: Taylor Swift Eras Tour ends with record-breaking impact
topics.summary: Taylor Swift wrapped her 21-month Eras Tour in late 2024, the highest-grossing concert tour in history. Economists tracked its impact on local hotel and restaurant economies in dozens of cities. Cultural critics debated what it meant that a single artist could become a city-by-city stimulus event.
searchTerms: Taylor Swift Eras Tour ends Vancouver

topics.event: Paris Olympics opening ceremony breaks the stadium format
topics.summary: The 2024 Paris Olympics opening ceremony took place along the Seine, the first to leave a stadium. Athletes paraded by boat past Notre-Dame and the Louvre while a torch-bearing relay involved Snoop Dogg and a hot-air balloon cauldron. Reviews were polarized.

topics.event: BookTok reshapes the publishing industry
topics.summary: TikTok's #BookTok community has driven millions of book sales and transformed how publishers acquire, market, and shelve titles. By 2024, romance and fantasy authors with viral TikTok presence have surpassed many traditionally promoted writers in physical bookstore sales.

topics.event: Climate protesters throw soup at the Mona Lisa
topics.summary: Two activists from a French climate group threw pumpkin soup at the Louvre's Mona Lisa in early 2024. The painting was unharmed behind protective glass. The act reopened debate about whether disruptive protest at famous artworks helps or hurts the climate cause.

topics.event: Hollywood writers and actors strikes leave a changed industry
topics.summary: After the 2023 WGA writers strike and SAG-AFTRA actors strike both ended, 2024 saw the resulting industry contraction: fewer scripted shows, AI-protection clauses in new contracts, and a generation of mid-career writers questioning whether to stay.

topics.event: Stanford president resigns over research integrity questions
topics.summary: Stanford University president Marc Tessier-Lavigne resigned after an internal review found that some scientific papers from his lab contained manipulated images. The case became a symbol of larger debates about research ethics and the pressure to publish.

topics.event: Australia caps international student visas
topics.summary: Australia announced a hard cap on international student visa approvals starting in 2025, citing housing pressure and migration concerns. Universities, which depend heavily on international tuition, warned of layoffs and program cuts.

topics.event: Canada limits international student permits
topics.summary: Canada introduced a two-year cap on international student permits in early 2024, reducing approvals by about 35 percent. The change was driven by housing strain in cities like Toronto and concerns about diploma mills.

topics.event: UK debates whether to keep its graduate work visa
topics.summary: The UK's Graduate Route, which lets international students stay and work for two years after graduation, came under review in 2024. Universities defended it as essential for international recruitment; the Migration Advisory Committee concluded it should stay.

topics.event: Total cost of US private college crosses $100,000 a year
topics.summary: Vanderbilt and several other top US private universities published 2024-25 sticker prices above $100,000 per year for tuition, room, and board combined. The threshold prompted public reckoning about who pays full price and whether the published number is meaningful.

topics.event: Universities settle into life with generative AI in essays
topics.summary: Two years after ChatGPT's release, universities have largely abandoned outright bans and instead reshaped assignments. In-class writing returned, oral defenses became more common, and AI-detection tools were quietly dropped after being shown to be unreliable.

topics.event: EU AI Act takes effect
topics.summary: The European Union's AI Act, the world's first comprehensive AI regulation, began phased enforcement in 2024. It bans certain uses outright (such as social scoring), tightly regulates high-risk applications, and requires transparency for general-purpose models.
searchTerms: EU AI Act high-risk systems transparency

topics.event: COP29 climate summit in Baku ends with contested finance deal
topics.summary: The 2024 UN climate summit in Azerbaijan agreed on a $300 billion-per-year finance commitment from rich countries to developing ones. Many developing countries called the figure inadequate, arguing the real need was several times higher.

topics.event: India holds the largest election in history
topics.summary: Almost 970 million Indians were eligible to vote in the 2024 general election, conducted in seven phases over six weeks. The result returned Narendra Modi to a third term but with a weaker mandate than in 2019, requiring coalition partners to govern.

topics.event: UK Labour returns to power after 14 years in opposition
topics.summary: Britain's Labour Party won a landslide majority in the July 2024 general election under Keir Starmer, ending 14 years of Conservative government. The campaign was unusually quiet for British politics, with the Conservatives campaigning largely on damage limitation.

topics.event: Japan hits record tourism numbers amid weak yen
topics.summary: Japan welcomed an all-time high of foreign visitors in 2024, driven partly by a historically weak yen. The boom is reshaping rural towns and stretching famous sites like Kyoto and Mount Fuji.

topics.event: Fourth global coral bleaching event recorded
topics.summary: NOAA confirmed in 2024 that the world has entered its fourth global mass coral bleaching event, the second in a decade. Reefs from Australia to the Caribbean are losing color and, in many places, life.
searchTerms: NOAA coral bleaching Great Barrier Reef

topics.event: First commercial octopus farm sparks ethics debate
topics.summary: A Spanish company's plan to open the world's first commercial octopus farm has run into international opposition from scientists and ethicists who argue octopuses are too cognitively complex to farm humanely.
searchTerms: Spain octopus farm Nueva Pescanova ethics
octopus farm regulation Washington state ban

topics.event: GLP-1 drugs found to affect more than weight
topics.summary: Studies in 2024 reported that GLP-1 receptor agonists like Ozempic appear to reduce alcohol cravings, slow kidney decline, and possibly lower dementia risk. Researchers are racing to understand why.

topics.event: Nepal raises Everest climbing permit fees
topics.summary: Nepal announced a major increase in permit fees for climbing Mount Everest, the first significant hike in nearly a decade. Officials say the change is meant to manage crowding and fund cleanup of the mountain.

topics.event: Finland tops World Happiness Report seven years running
topics.summary: The 2024 World Happiness Report named Finland the world's happiest country for the seventh consecutive year. Researchers point to social trust, low corruption, and a culture that values modesty.

topics.event: Notre-Dame reopens after five-year restoration
topics.summary: Paris's Notre-Dame cathedral reopened in December 2024, five years after a devastating fire. The restoration brought together stonemasons, glassworkers, and carpenters trained in medieval methods.
searchTerms: Notre Dame restoration craftsmanship medieval techniques
Notre Dame fire five years later

topics.event: Iceland's volcanic eruptions become routine
topics.summary: After a series of repeated eruptions on the Reykjanes peninsula starting in 2023, Iceland has had to develop new infrastructure and tourism approaches to live alongside ongoing volcanic activity.

topics.event: 4-day work week trials show productivity gains
topics.summary: Multiple large-scale 4-day work week trials, including in the UK and Iceland, reported sustained productivity and improved well-being. Some participating companies have made the change permanent.
searchTerms: four day week productivity UK Iceland

topics.event: Mexico City sinks as aquifers drain
topics.summary: Mexico City is sinking by up to 50 centimeters per year in some neighborhoods as overdrawn aquifers compact below the surface. The city is debating water rationing, rainwater harvesting, and managed retreat.

topics.event: Ocean Cleanup hits 10 million kg milestone
topics.summary: The Ocean Cleanup nonprofit reported in 2024 that it has removed over 10 million kilograms of plastic from oceans and rivers. Critics question whether the approach scales, but supporters call it the largest ocean cleanup ever attempted.
searchTerms: Ocean Cleanup Great Pacific Garbage Patch 2024
